line_1: For a chap at the door in braid day-light,
line_2: Yet the auld gudewife and her mays sae tight,

line_1: But a wooer that comes in braid day-light,
line_2: His coat it was new, and his o'erlay was white,

line_1: And almost faints with joy amid the broad daylight.
line_2: She thinks her long-lost friends shall bless her sight,
